forecasts.reasoning: As of June 15, 2025, Donald Trump accepted an invitation to visit China during a June 5 phone call with Xi Jinping. However, there has been no official date set, and Trump mentioned that "respective teams will be meeting shortly" to figure out logistics. Bilateral delegations met in London between June 8 and June 13 to discuss trade and potentially lay the groundwork. Organizing a US presidential trip to China is a complex logistical and security operation that typically requires several weeks or months of advance planning. Because only 15 days remain between the forecast date (June 15, 2025) and the resolution date (July 1, 2025), a surprise trip within this short window is extremely unlikely. Therefore, the probability of the visit happening before July 1, 2025, is very low.
